  1. Home
  2. Writing
  3. Articles overview

Articles

Articles and essays

Longer-form writing about modern CSS architecture, maintainability, performance, accessibility, and how CSS methodologies have changed as the platform has improved.

Start here

CSS methodology evolution

Understand why older methodologies emerged, what problems they solved, and where modern CSS changes the trade-offs.

CSS @scope and LSCSS

Explore how @scope fits into Layered Semantic CSS and how it can reduce selector noise without replacing architecture.

Reading paths

Methodology thinking

Start with methodology evolution, then compare LSCSS against BEM, CUBE, and scoped CSS in the comparison guides.

View comparisons

Examples and patterns

Read the @scope article, then use before-and-after examples for selectors, utilities, state, and migration decisions.

View examples

Practical adoption

Pair the articles with migration guidance, examples, and the starter template when applying LSCSS to real projects.

View migration guidance

Future article ideas

  • Why cascade layers change CSS architecture
  • Why specificity problems are often process problems
  • How to migrate legacy CSS without a rewrite
  • How modern CSS affects BEM-style naming
  • Accessibility as part of CSS architecture
  • Performance, bundle size, and unused CSS at scale

LSCSS

Layered Semantic CSS. A practical methodology for calm, maintainable front-end architecture.

Evolves with real delivery. Change what does not fit your codebase.
